reasoning: Rule 1 states final voiced stops become devoiced. Sentence d shows 'neet' (need), 'bat' (bad), 'ant' (and), 'lap' (lab) where final d→t and b→p.
Rule 2 states doubled consonants become single. Sentence c shows 'realy' (really), 'carots' (carrots), 'dolars' (dollars), 'masive' (massive), 'trilion' (trillion) where ll→l, rr→r, ss→s.
Rule 3 states s becomes z at word beginning. Sentence e shows all s-initial words now starting with z: 'zpilled', 'zynthetic', 'zuperfood', 'zyrup', 'zmart-zuit'.
Rule 4 states vowels following vowels are deleted. Sentence a shows 'soked' (soaked), 'floting' (floating), 'osis' (oasis) with vowel sequences reduced.

Rule 5 states e before consonants becomes a. Sentence b shows 'Aggplant' (Eggplant), 'fual' (fuel), 'axcallant' (excellent), 'anargy' (energy), 'afficiancy' (efficiency), 'battar' (better), 'alactric' (electric).
Language A has rules about e-deletion and ei formation, characteristic of Portuguese. Portuguese shows nasalization and specific vowel evolution patterns.
Language B has rules about e-deletion before s+consonant clusters, and Italian preserves more conservative features including doubled consonants.
Language C has the ll→dd rule which is uniquely characteristic of Sardinian, seen in cabaddu, casteddu, puddu, bidda.
The rule h→Ø shows H drops word-initially, as in HABERE→avere.
Intervocalic b weakens to v in Vulgar Latin, as seen in CIBUS→civu.
Intervocalic b becomes v, part of general lenition processes in Vulgar Latin.
Long vowels change quality before L, part of vowel restructuring.
Long ū shortens and may change quality in this context.
Latin long ē merges with other vowels to become e in Vulgar Latin.
Velar k has different evolution in Sardinian path vs Western Romance.
Sardinian preserves velar k more conservatively.
In Western Romance, k palatalizes before front vowels.
k becomes affricate č before front vowels in Western Romance (later becoming different sounds in daughter languages).
The palatalization occurs before front (non-back) vowels like e and i.
Single n may double after vowels in certain contexts.
Consonant gemination (doubling) after short vowels.
Vowel changes in Portuguese development.
Back vowel raising o→u in specific contexts.
In Portuguese, final e is deleted (word-final position).
Latin e becomes ei diphthong in Portuguese in certain contexts.
Latin au diphthong changes in Italian.
au monophthongizes to o in Italian.
This change occurs in all positions (unconditional).
In Sardinian, v becomes b, as in VIRDEM→birde.
Sardinian changes v to b, opposite of the general Romance trend.
The distinctive Sardinian change: ll→dd (CABALLUM→cabaddu, CASTELLUM→casteddu, PULLUM→puddu, VĪLLA→bidda).
BACULUM through Latin→VL→WR changes: -UM→-u, vowel adjustments, yields baculo.
VĪTA through changes: Ī→i, intervocalic T→D in Western Romance yields vida.
FINDĀRE through changes: -ĀRE→-are, Ā→a, yields findar in Western Romance.
